seo_description: Current local time in Bangkok, Thailand. ICT (UTC+7) year-round, no daylight saving time. SE Asia's largest city. Live clock, Bangkok vs London & Singapore.

faq.question: What time is it in Bangkok right now?
faq.answer: Bangkok, Thailand uses Indochina Time (ICT, UTC+7) year-round. Thailand does not observe Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is Asia/Bangkok. Bangkok operates on Indochina Time (ICT/UTC+7) year-round. Thailand does not observe daylight saving time, so Bangkok's UTC+7 offset remains constant throughout the year. This makes Bangkok 7 hours ahead of London (GMT), 12 hours ahead of New York (EST), and 1 hour behind Singapore and Hong Kong.

faq.question: What time zone is Bangkok in?
faq.answer: Bangkok is in the Indochina Time (ICT) zone, which is UTC+7 year-round. ICT is shared by Thailand, Vietnam, Cambodia, Laos, and parts of Indonesia. It sits between China Standard Time (UTC+8) and India Standard Time (UTC+5:30), making Bangkok a useful mid-point for scheduling between South Asian and East Asian partners.

faq.question: Does Bangkok observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Thailand has never observed daylight saving time. Bangkok maintains Indochina Time (ICT/UTC+7) year-round, providing consistent and predictable scheduling for international business. This stability is a practical advantage for regional companies coordinating across multiple Asian time zones.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Bangkok and London?
faq.answer: Bangkok is 7 hours ahead of London during GMT (October–March) and 6 hours ahead during British Summer Time (BST, March–October). When it is 9:00 AM in London (GMT), it is 4:00 PM in Bangkok. The best window for London–Bangkok calls is early London morning: 8:00–10:00 AM GMT (3:00–5:00 PM Bangkok), catching Bangkok before end of business.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Bangkok and Dubai?
faq.answer: Bangkok is 3 hours ahead of Dubai (GST/UTC+4) year-round. Both cities do not observe daylight saving time, so this 3-hour difference is constant throughout the year. When it is 9:00 AM in Dubai, it is 12:00 PM noon in Bangkok — making the midday window ideal for Gulf–Thailand business coordination.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Bangkok and Singapore?
faq.answer: Bangkok is 1 hour behind Singapore. Singapore operates on SGT (UTC+8) while Bangkok uses ICT (UTC+7). When it is 10:00 AM in Bangkok, it is 11:00 AM in Singapore. Despite being geographically close, the 1-hour gap means Singapore's business day starts and ends an hour earlier relative to Bangkok.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Bangkok from the US?
faq.answer: From New York, Bangkok is 12 hours ahead during EST (November–March), making direct business overlap extremely difficult. The closest window is early morning New York (8:00–10:00 AM EST = 8:00–10:00 PM Bangkok). From Los Angeles (PST), Bangkok is 15 hours ahead — making evening PST (6:00–8:00 PM) the most practical for same-day contact.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Bangkok?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Bangkok are 8:30 AM to 5:30 PM ICT, Monday through Friday. Government offices operate 8:30 AM–4:30 PM. Banks are generally open 10:00 AM–5:00 PM on weekdays. Many international companies in Bangkok adopt flexible hours to accommodate calls with European or American head offices, often starting earlier or finishing later.
